name: Moggy
recipeInstructions:
1. Mix together the flour, salt and baking powder.
2. Rub in the fats, add the sugar and syrup.
3. Mix to a stiff dough with milk.
4. Shape into two pieces 1-inch thick.
5. Place on a greased baking tin and bake in a moderate oven for about 25 minutes, until firm and light brown.
6. Cut and serve thickly buttered.
Remarks:
This recipe can be found in many very old books, and may have come from the Old Norse language, where a heap of corn was known as Mugi. The word can be traced back through early English, where corn was referred to as Muge and later Muga. In its earliest form, it was probably made with yeast and honey instead of baking powder and syrup/sugar.
